commit.message:
refactor(frontend): simplify workspace rendering and data flow

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: comprehensive code review - 80+ fixes across all backend files

Critical fixes:
- Rewrite subscribe.py: generate client outbound configs (was server inbound)
- Fix UUID generation: use uuid4() instead of token_urlsafe (sing-box requirement)
- Fix Shadowsocks 2022 key: base64-encoded 16-byte key for aes-128-gcm
- Fix empty urltest crash: guard against empty outbound_tags
- Replace deprecated geoip route rules with rule_set + SRS files
- Fix transport tcp: omit transport field for TCP (sing-box default)
- Fix Docker sing-box config path: add explicit command override
- Fix systemd PATH: include full system PATH in Environment

Security fixes:
- Add auth middleware (ADMIN_API_KEY via Bearer/X-API-Key)
- Add registration secret (REGISTRATION_SECRET)
- Use hmac.compare_digest for all token comparisons (timing attack)
- Authenticate script download endpoint
- Set 0o600 permissions on agent config files
- Add CORS origin restriction support
- Add startup security warnings

New features:
- API pagination on all list endpoints (?page=&per_page=)
- Database indexes on frequently queried columns
- Protocol templates and deployment scripts
- Docker HEALTHCHECK
- Example configuration

Bug fixes:
- Fix AI proxy selector referencing non-existent outbound
- Fix naive protocol missing username field
- Fix race condition on user creation (TOCTOU)
- Validate node_ids types in batch commands
- Fix report_health missing query-param auth fallback
- Replace subprocess curl with requests in agent
- Quote shell variables in install.sh
- Fix Clash TLS gaps (trojan, tuic)
- Complete Surge config rewrite with proxy definitions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: complete first-run setup wizard backend

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: first-run setup wizard — integration complete

- cargo build, cargo test (47/47), cargo clippy -D warnings all pass
- npm run build and npx vitest run (55/55) all pass
- Update .gitignore to exclude .claude/ agent state directory
- Add AGENTS.md with GitNexus code intelligence instructions
- Update CLAUDE.md with GitNexus workflow section
- Add first-run-setup-wizard-spec.md feature specification

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: apply council fixes for pr-31-first-run-setup-wizard

- rename _secrets -> secrets in SshTunnel::connect; fail fast with
  actionable error when passphrase-protected key is used (openssh has
  no passphrase API; direct users to ssh-add instead)
- replace KnownHosts::Accept with KnownHosts::Add (TOFU: accept on
  first connect, reject if host key changes) to prevent MITM
- add TOCTOU comment on ephemeral port allocation window
- add mode guard to save_config: returns error if AppMode::Normal
  to prevent post-setup config overwrite
- add mode guard to test_connection: same guard for consistency

Fixes: SSH passphrase bug, KnownHosts MITM (high), setup endpoint
security (medium), test_connection hardening (improvement),
TOCTOU documentation (low)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(setup): 409 on mode guards, secrets cleanup on error, 409 test coverage

- test_connection and save_config now return HTTP 409 Conflict (not 200)
  when the mode guard fires (setup already complete). Return types changed
  from Json<T> to impl IntoResponse; all branches call .into_response().

- save_config steps 8 and 9 now also remove .seeki.secrets alongside
  seeki.toml when rolling back a failed write cycle, preventing stale
  secrets files from persisting on disk.

- Added two tokio tests (test_connection_rejects_when_not_in_setup_mode,
  save_config_rejects_when_not_in_setup_mode) using test_router_with_mode
  with a lazy-connected AppMode::Normal to assert HTTP 409 status.
  No live database required.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: apply third council blocking fixes for PR #31

B1: Remove hardcoded server config from SetupStep4Confirm.svelte
- Removed `server: { host: 'localhost', port: 8080 }` from the
  setupSaveConfig call body in SetupStep4Confirm.svelte:45.
- The backend's SaveServerConfig fields have serde(default) annotations
  (config.rs:96-101) that produce the correct 127.0.0.1:3141 bind address
  when the key is absent. Sending localhost:8080 was overriding those
  defaults and silently misconfiguring every wizard save.

B2: Clean up .seeki.secrets on chmod failure in setup.rs
- Added `let _ = std::fs::remove_file(".seeki.secrets");` at setup.rs:317,
  before the existing seeki.toml removal in the write_secrets_file error path.
- If set_permissions(0o600) fails after fs::write, the secrets file was
  previously left world-readable permanently. Now both files are removed
  consistently, matching the cleanup at L:329-330 and L:343-344.

Also adds third council recommendation file.

All 49 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: apply fourth council fixes for PR #31

- Add #[serde(default)] to SaveConfigRequest.server so requests that
  omit the 'server' key (as Step4Confirm now does) don't cause a 422
- Add derive(Default) to SaveServerConfig to support serde(default)
- Add password field to SshWizardConfig backend struct and wire it
  through wizard_to_ssh (fixes SSH password auth field naming)
- Fix SSH password input binding in SetupStep1Connection.svelte:
  was binding to key_passphrase, now correctly binds to password
- Add password: '' to wizardData.ssh init in SetupWizard.svelte
- Add password?: string to frontend SshWizardConfig type in types.ts
- Mark SetupSaveRequest.server optional in types.ts to match backend
- Replace no-op Drop impl in SshTunnel with a clarifying comment
  (openssh::Session's own Drop closes the ControlMaster connection)
- Add regression test: save_config_accepts_request_without_server_field

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: apply fifth council fixes for PR #31

F1/F2: Add AbortController with 60s timeout to setupTestConnection and
setupSaveConfig — SSH tunnel negotiation needs longer than the default 30s.
S1: Add 'ssh_config' to error_source union in TestConnectionResult.
S2: Remove empty Drop impl; suppress dead_code on session field (kept for RAII).

* fix: apply sixth council fixes for PR #31

* docs: add council session files for PR #31 review history

---------

Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: Cancel button in EditProfileDialog now closes the dialog

The onDismiss callback was calling selectProfile(profile), which re-set
_editingProfile to the same object — the dialog stayed open. Replace
with a new dismissEdit() method that sets _editingProfile to null.

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Docs versioning: Add glob support, better validation and reporting (#3278)

* Docs versioning: Add glob support, better validation and reporting

Normalize CLI target specs (handle Windows/backslashes, ./, absolute paths) and classify them as file/dir/glob. Implement matching logic and validation (report matched files and unmatched selectors, return rc=2 on unmatched), and apply target specs in scan/update. Add helpers (normalize_target_spec, compile_target_specs, validate_requested_targets, print_target_match_summary, iter_scan_candidate_paths) and adjust main argument help. Update tests to cover directory, glob, Windows-style paths, and CLI reporting.

* Handle invalid target specs in validation

Treat target specs that fail normalization as kind "invalid" so they are not silently ignored. compile_target_specs now appends invalid specs, target_spec_matches_path returns False for invalid kinds, and validate_requested_targets records invalid raw selectors as unmatched (and safely iterates when specs may be None). This ensures malformed or non-normalizable CLI selectors are reported back to the user rather than dropped.

* tests: add repo/cfg fixtures and refactor tests

Introduce shared pytest fixtures (repo, cfg) and import Callable to reduce repetition of tmp_path/git init logic across tests. Refactor many tests to use the new fixtures and ToolConfig factory, and add/adjust tests covering target validation and scanning edge cases (several validate_requested_targets variations, scan_files with invalid only-targets, and main returning 2 for invalid selector). Overall this centralizes repo/config setup and adds coverage for target handling behavior.

* Clarify --targets help text

Expand and clarify the --targets argument help strings in tools/docs_and_notebooks_check.py for the update and normalize subcommands. The updated messages document that --targets accepts exact files, directories, and glob patterns (with examples) and note that both '/' and '\\' path separators are accepted. This is a documentation-only change to improve user guidance; no functional behavior is altered.

* Update tools/docs_and_notebooks_check.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update docs_and_notebooks_check.py

* Revert "Merge branch 'cy/docs-versioning-tweaks' of https://github.com/DeepLabCut/DeepLabCut into cy/docs-versioning-tweaks"

This reverts commit 46fdb042e13eb7a968b0a1c4da0a6681cd9ef75a, reversing
changes made to 6d3b1be82255da0f832a4b47e0732507d52305d3.

* Use TypedDict for targets and simplify glob matching

Introduce TypedDict-based types for FileKind and TargetSpec (with TargetKind) and tighten type annotations across functions (compile_target_specs, target_spec_matches_path, target_matches). Replace PurePosixPath-based glob matching with fnmatch.fnmatchcase only to ensure consistent, shell-style pattern behavior across platforms and remove unused imports. Minor cleanups: import TypedDict and update variable type hints for better static checking and readability.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Switch to javax servlet APIs and Jetty 10

Replace Jakarta EE 5 servlet/JSP artifacts with Javax equivalents and target Servlet/JSP 4.0: update pom.xml to use javax.servlet:javax.servlet-api 4.0.1 and javax.servlet.jsp:javax.servlet.jsp-api 2.3.3, and downgrade jetty-maven-plugin from 11.0.20 to 10.0.20. fix: generate GitHub App token for create-pull-request and auto-merge steps (#55)

The collect-metrics workflow was failing because secrets.METRICS_GITHUB_TOKEN
was not supplied to peter-evans/create-pull-request@v8. Fix by generating a
short-lived token from the existing GitHub App credentials (APP_ID +
APP_PRIVATE_KEY) and using that token in the Create PR and Enable auto-merge
steps.

feat: FLAC passthrough + pre-buffer for reduced playback latency

- Eliminate local ffmpeg transcoding: raw audio bytes (FLAC/MP3/AAC)
  pass directly from Yandex CDN to MA's ffmpeg
- Pre-buffer system: audio download starts on Ynison track change
  before the player HTTP GET, hiding API/CDN latency
- PluginSource audio_format changed from PCM_S16LE to FLAC
- Seek uses ffmpeg fallback only when needed
- Reduces playback start delay by ~3-5s (15-20s → 10-12s)
- 80 tests passing, 9 new tests for passthrough and pre-buffer

Co-authored-by: OmniQuestMediaInc <240893763+OmniQuestMediaInc@users.noreply.github.com>

chore(tooling): Split publish into validate + sdk + extension jobs

Validation (typecheck, tests, build) is separated from publishing to ensure
that only verified artifacts are published. Splitting SDK and extension
publishing into distinct jobs makes failures clearer, allows different
publish-time configuration (e.g., avoiding caches to ensure clean builds),
and enables generating per-package SBOMs for traceability. This improves
supply-chain hygiene and auditing without changing overall release semantics.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Alexandre NICOLAIE <alexandre.nicolaie.com>

chore(tooling): Add YAML schema hint to workflows

validation and autocompletion for GitHub Actions workflow files. This
improves developer productivity, reduces misconfigurations, and makes
workflow files easier to author and review. The change is non-invasive and
improves the DX for contributors.

commercial product for expired tokens. Not a refund. Not a grace period.
Extension fee framing only.

Token Bridge mechanic is repurposed as Option A of the Token Extension product.
Step 2 (Three-Fifths Exit cash refund) and flagAfterDeclinedOffers are eliminated.
No cash refund path exists on the platform.

Class renamed TokenExtensionService. offerThreeFifthsExit method removed.
flagAfterDeclinedOffers method removed. DisputeStage enum simplified.
offerTokenBridge method retained and reframed as Option A extension pathway.
All internal references to THREE_FIFTHS_REFUND_PCT removed.

commit.message:
chore(tooling): Publish both SDK and extension in release workflow

The release workflow previously published only the SDK package, requiring
manual steps to publish the extension and risking inconsistency between
packages. Publishing both the SDK and the root extension from a single
release provides a single authoritative release process with consistent
provenance attestation and reduces human error while keeping release
automation straightforward and auditable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Alexandre NICOLAIE <alexandre.nicolaie.com>

commit.message:
refactor: Complete redesign of auth with dropdown menu and separate flows

- Add 'Sign Up / Login' button in top-right corner with dropdown menu
- Dropdown shows 2 options: Mentee Sign Up/Login OR Mentor Sign Up/Login
- Completely separate pages for mentee and mentor (no tabs/sections)
- URL parameter: /auth.html?role=mentor goes directly to mentor login
- Each role has distinct visual styling and badge colors
- Mentee: Blue gradient, Mentor: Gold/amber badge
- Independent login/signup flows for mentee and mentor
- Mentee pages: login, signup, dashboard, bookings, profile
- Mentor pages: login, signup, dashboard (placeholder)
- Dropdown menu closes when user selects a role
- Better UX: cleaner navigation, separate concerns

Changes:
- Replaced tab-based design with dropdown menu approach
- Separated mentee and mentor into distinct page flows
- Added initMentee() and initMentor() functions for role switching
- Index.html buttons still point to /auth.html?role=mentor

commit.message:
feat: intelligent RSS load balancing across multiple qBT instances

- Add load_balancer.py: selects least-loaded instance by scoring
  (active torrents, upload speed, paused count)
- Add rss_poller.py: server-side RSS polling with filter matching
  and load-balanced instance selection
- Add RSSProcessedItem model to track processed RSS entries
- Update RSS API: instance_id defaults to 0 (auto/load balanced)
- Update frontend: '自动（负载均衡）' as default instance option
- Add feedparser dependency for server-side RSS parsing
- Rewrite README: remove privacy data, add Mermaid diagrams,
  document load balancing strategy

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Greens in Regulation (GIR) stat throughout the app

GIR formula: a hole is GIR when (score - putts) <= (par - 2)
  e.g. Par 4, score 5, 3 putts → 5-3=2 ≤ 4-2=2 → GIR ✓

Changes:
- calcGIR() helper computes hits, total and pct for a set of holes
- History cards: show GIR% per round alongside putts
- Stats summary: two new cards — GIR Overall % and GIR Last 5 (rolling avg)
- Stats charts: new GIR % Per Round line chart (last 10 rounds, 0-100% Y axis)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(theme): fix dark/light toggle — default to dark, consistent across screens (#510)

Fixes #491

## Changes
- Changed default theme preference from SYSTEM to DARK in UserPreferences.kt
- Updated MainActivity initial value to match DARK default
- Replaced hardcoded colors in SettingsScreen with MaterialTheme.colorScheme:
  - Color.White → MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurface
  - Color(0xFF9E9E9E) → MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant
  - Ensures theme changes properly affect all screens

## Testing
- App now defaults to dark theme on first install
- Theme toggle works consistently across all screens
- No hardcoded light colors preventing theme changes

commit.message:
feat: update StreamingService for multi-protocol streaming

Replace lazy EncoderBridge with per-session nullable bridge created via
ProtocolAwareBridgeFactory. Add buildConnectionParams() to construct
typed Rtmp/Srt params from EndpointProfile. Add buildEncoderConfig()
to snapshot encoder settings from SettingsRepository flows.

Validate codec/protocol compatibility before stream start (rejects AV1
over SRT). Bridge is released on stop and recreated for each session.

commit.message:
Optimize full (and light/medium) index creation

Three independent optimizations, all in indexing.py.

Stage 1: replace per-run np.lexsort with the native Cython
intra_chunk_sort_run kernel in _build_full_descriptor_ooc().
Eliminates the np.arange allocation, the O(N log N) lexsort, and
two gather passes in favour of the GIL-free stable mergesort.

Stage 2: replace _merge_sorted_slices (concat + lexsort, O(N log N)
linear merge, GIL-free) inside _merge_run_pair().

Stage 3: rewrite _build_levels_descriptor_ooc() with a single-pass
strategy.  Previously the function issued one array[start:stop]
decompression call per segment across all levels (e.g. 9008 calls for
10M rows on a Mac M4: 8 chunk + 1000 block + 8000 subblock).  The new
code decompresses each array chunk exactly once and computes all level
summaries in a single vectorized pass via _fill_summaries_from_2d(),
falling back to the original per-segment path only when segment sizes
do not divide the chunk length evenly.

Measured on 10M random float64:

  Mac mini M4 (chunks=1.25M, blocks=10K):
    light:  940 ms ->  431 ms  (2.2x)
    medium: 4696 ms -> 394 ms (11.9x)
    full:   9650 ms -> 1584 ms  (6.1x)

  AMD 7800X3D Linux (chunks=2M, blocks=20K):
    light:   603 ms -> 430 ms  (1.4x)
    medium: 2050 ms -> 395 ms  (5.2x)
    full:   6856 ms -> 1582 ms  (4.3x)

Query latency and index sizes are unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
